name: Community Online Tools (COT)
description: Comprehensive server administration mod with in-game tools for managing players, objects, and more.
overview:
Community Online Tools (COT) is the go-to admin toolkit for DayZ servers. It provides in-game interfaces for managing players, spawning items and objects, teleporting, and adjusting server settings in real-time.

COT uses CF's permission system for granular access control — you can grant specific admin tools to different staff roles.
installation: Subscribe to Community Online Tools on the Steam Workshop.
Place the @CommunityOnlineTools folder in your server's root directory.
Both clients and servers must have this mod loaded.
Grant yourself admin permissions by adding your Steam64 ID to the permissions file or using the in-game console.

troubleshooting.solution: Ensure you have admin permissions. Try pressing INSERT or the configured keybind.
Add your Steam64 ID to the superadmin list in the CF permissions file.

useCases.title: Server Administration
useCases.description: Use COT for day-to-day server management — spawning items, teleporting players, adjusting weather.

useCases.title: Base Inspection
useCases.description: Admin camera and ESP tools let you inspect player bases without physically traveling.

useCases.description: Grant specific tools to different staff roles using granular permission nodes.

Community Online Tools (COT) is the go-to admin toolkit for DayZ servers. It provides in-game interfaces for managing players, spawning items and objects, teleporting, and adjusting server settings in real-time.

COT uses CF's permission system for granular access control — you can grant specific admin tools to different staff roles.

steps: Subscribe to Community Online Tools on the Steam Workshop.
Place the @Community-Online-Tools folder in your server's root directory.
Both clients and servers must have this mod loaded.
The mod is now installed. Please watch the Guide to learn how to set yourself admin

useCases.title: COT menu not appearing
useCases.description: Ensure you have admin permissions. Try pressing INSERT or the configured keybind.

useCases.title: Cannot grant permissions
useCases.description: Add your Steam64 ID to the superadmin list in the CF permissions file.
